react.name: React
react.description: Using components as the fundamental unit, React enables predictable updates and high-performance rendering through declarative rendering and Fiber scheduling, ideal for structured development of medium to large frontend systems
highlights: Build composable, reusable UI abstraction layers based on component models
Fiber architecture supports task splitting and priority scheduling, enhancing rendering performance and responsiveness
Mature ecosystem (routing, state management, SSR), suitable for engineering system construction

vue.name: Vue
vue.description: Through reactive data-driven view updates, combined with template syntax and Composition API, Vue balances development efficiency with system maintainability, suitable for progressive construction from simple pages to complex applications
highlights: Reactive dependency tracking mechanism for fine-grained updates and intuitive data flow
Single File Components (SFC) enhance development consistency and module cohesion
Unified ecosystem (Vue Router / Pinia), ideal for rapid delivery and team collaboration

javascript.name: JavaScript
javascript.description: As the core language of the Web platform, JavaScript covers event loops, prototype inheritance, closures, and asynchronous programming models, serving as the foundation for understanding browser runtime mechanisms and building complex interactive systems
highlights: Single-threaded concurrency model driven by Event Loop
Coexistence of functional and object-oriented paradigms, supporting flexible abstractions
Unified language foundation spanning frontend, server-side (Node.js), and build toolchains

frontendEngineering.name: Frontend Engineering
frontendEngineering.description: Centered on modularization, build optimization, and development process automation, establishing a stable and efficient frontend engineering system to address code organization, performance optimization, and team collaboration challenges
highlights: Module bundling and build optimization based on Vite / Webpack (Code Splitting / Tree Shaking)
Unified code standards (Lint / Format) and quality assurance (Testing / CI/CD)
Engineering implementation capabilities supporting Monorepo, Micro-frontend, and other complex architectures

nodejs.name: Node.js
nodejs.description: A server-side runtime based on event-driven and non-blocking I/O, Node.js not only supports backend service development but also serves as the core foundation for frontend build toolchains and automation processes
highlights: Event-driven + async I/O model, suitable for high-concurrency scenarios
Indispensable as frontend toolchain runtime (build, scaffolding, CLI)
Rich ecosystem (npm), supporting full-stack development from script tools to server applications
